successCriteria.description: src/shared/types/index.ts exists and exports a LeaveType enum with exactly three members: ANNUAL, SICK, EMERGENCY
src/shared/types/index.ts exports a LeaveRequestStatus enum with exactly six members: DRAFT, SUBMITTED, APPROVED, REJECTED, CANCELLED, REVOKED
src/shared/types/index.ts exports a BalanceStatus enum with exactly three members: ACTIVE, EXHAUSTED, FROZEN
tests/unit/shared/types.spec.ts exists with unit tests that verify enum value membership for all three enums (LeaveType, LeaveRequestStatus, BalanceStatus)

constraints: TypeScript strict mode: no implicit any, strict null checks (from HARNESS.json stack description)
No `any` type — use `unknown` with type guards instead (HARNESS.json constraint rule no-any)
Enums must be importable from the single public entry point src/shared/types/index.ts (ARCHITECTURE.md dependency rule: modules import only from declared public entry points)
outOfScope: Phase 2: LeavePolicy model and repository (src/modules/policy/policy.model.ts, policy.repository.ts)
Phase 3: LeaveRequest model and repository (src/modules/leave/leave.model.ts, leave.repository.ts)
Phase 4: LeaveBalance model and repository (src/modules/balance/balance.model.ts, balance.repository.ts)
Phase 5: LeavePolicy service (IPolicyService interface + PolicyService implementation)
Phase 6: Leave application service (ILeaveService interface + LeaveService with business rules)
Phase 7: Policy routes (Fastify route handlers for policy CRUD)
Phase 8: Leave routes (Fastify route handlers for leave request lifecycle)
Phase 9: Balance service and routes (IBalanceService + BalanceService + balance routes)
Phase 10: Database migrations for leave domain tables (Knex migrations for leave_policies, leave_requests, leave_balances)
Any domain models, repositories, services, controllers, routes, or database migrations beyond the three shared enums and their unit test

ambiguities.description: Test framework mismatch: HARNESS.json declares "testFramework": "Jest" and the intent says "Jest unit test", but AGENTS.md and ARCHITECTURE.md both declare "Test framework: Vitest". The test file at tests/unit/shared/types.spec.ts must use one framework consistently.
options: Use Jest (as stated in HARNESS.json and the intent)
Use Vitest (as stated in AGENTS.md and ARCHITECTURE.md)

ambiguities.description: Enum value style not specified: the intent does not declare whether LeaveType, LeaveRequestStatus, and BalanceStatus should be TypeScript string enums (e.g., ANNUAL = 'ANNUAL') or numeric enums (e.g., ANNUAL = 0). String enums are more conventional for database-backed domain values and produce more readable serialized output.
